items.title: 'Only a little baby': Family of missing 5yo Sharon Granites speaks

children.content: Family members of a missing Alice Springs five-year-old have spoken to the ABC for the first time, pleading for anyone with information that could help find their "energetic" little girl to come forward.

articles.title: Police reveal items seized from crime scene in missing 5yo investigation

children.content: Northern Territory Police say some people in the community "absolutely know" the whereabouts of five-year-old girl Sharon Granites's suspected abductor, and are imploring anyone with information that could help find them to come forward.

image.altText: Tall grass with searchers in the background

articles.title: As search for missing 5yo Sharon continues, a clearer picture of that night emerges

children.content: Five-year-old Sharon Granites vanished from an Alice Springs town camp late on Saturday night, with police alleging she has been abducted. Here's what we know about the night she went missing.

image.altText: young girl in blue shirt sits in chair holding arms out front

gallery.altText: Young girl in pink dress does peace sign with fingers

thumbnail.altText: Young girl in pink dress does peace sign with fingers

items.title: Hanson billed taxpayers for flights to fundraiser, to catch Florida jet

children.content: One Nation leader Pauline Hanson billed taxpayers for two trips that included party fundraisers, discussions with disaffected National Barnaby Joyce, a Gina Rinehart event and return flights to connect the senator to her private jet to Florida, sponsored by her major donor, Mrs Rinehart.

image.altText: Pauline Hanson

items.title: China to help Australia with jet fuel supplies, Wong says

children.content: Australia's foreign minister says the Chinese Government is engaging with Australian businesses on jet fuel supplies as she tours the region.

image.altText: A woman stands in front of the Australian flag.

items.title: UK police declare terrorist incident after two Jewish men stabbed in London

children.content: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer has called the attack "deeply concerning" and said those reponsible would be brought to justice.

items.title: Inflation details may give RBA pause for thought on interest rates

children.content: While inflation remains well above the Reserve Bank's target, there are details in the numbers that may give it reasons to hold off another rate hike in May.

image.altText: A dark-haired woman in glasses casts her gaze downward as she stands at a lectern in forrnt of an RBA backdrop.

items.title: 'Extremely corrosive': Trump called out for remarks aimed at Australia

children.content: US congressman Joe Courtney says the US president's repeated criticism of Australia is "unfair" and causing harm to the US amongst its allies.

image.altText: Trump, wearing a bowtie, points at someone off camera

items.title: Two killed after light plane crashes into hangar in Adelaide's north

children.content: Two people are dead and a "number of others" have injuries after a plane crash at Parafield Airport in Adelaide's north.

items.title: US Supreme Court curbs race-based voting maps in landmark ruling

children.content: The ruling will be seen by civil rights activists as a blow and by the Republican party as a boost ahead of key midterm elections in November.

items.title: Australia's richest woman gifts plane to Pauline Hanson

children.content: One Nation leader Pauline Hanson banks more than $3 million worth of donations, including a brand-new plane gifted by Australia's richest person, Gina Rinehart.

image.altText: A woman wearing whtie pants and red and white shirts steps onto the wing of a grey plane.

items.title: King's speech to Congress hits on several Trump controversies

children.content: In a speech that was supposed to be apolitical, King Charles hits on some hot-button issues that are fuelling controversies around the Trump administration.

articles.title: Former FBI director charged over alleged social media photo threat to Trump

children.content: Former FBI director James Comey has been charged over a social media photo of seashells that officials say constituted a threat against US President Donald Trump.

image.altText: Comey gestures with both hands as he speaks on a stage.

articles.title: US regulator orders review of TV licence after Trump criticises Kimmel

children.content: It comes after both Donald and Melania Trump demanded the network cancel Kimmel's late-night comedy show over a joke they described as a call to violence, days before an alleged attempt to assassinate the US president.

items.title: Longest-serving WA MP, war hero Bill Grayden, dies at 105

items.title: Major childcare operator reveals list of 40 centres to close

children.content: Victoria and Western Australia will be affected the most, with 12 centres shutting in each state. NSW, Queensland and South Australia will also have closures.

items.title: What is OPEC? Why is the UAE leaving it?

items.title: Rebel Wilson accused of dumping her mobile to avoid handing over key texts

items.title: Police killer Dezi Freeman had hundreds of child abuse files
